title: adrs #19の判断来歴JSONLをpure projectionで検査する
JSONL欠落・dangling edge・stale digest・unknownをfail-closedにする

text: adrs #19 proposes a traceable decision log gate: background, claim, assessment, decision, and expected-outcome rows are required for ADR-changing proposals, while total score remains non-authority projection output.
governance/gov* is the side-effect-free projector/linter/compiler layer. DuckDB projection proof belongs here, while adrs keeps the decision and ops/evidence keeps runtime receipts.

statement: DuckDB SQL/view proof for the traceable decision log should be implemented in governance because it is pure projection and validation, not a runtime effect.
falsifier: The proof writes external state, requires credentials, or produces runtime receipts.

statement: The proof must show valid JSONL projects to trace coverage 100%, and invalid JSONL fails for missing rows, dangling edges, direct total score storage, non-determinism, and stale/missing references.
falsifier: A bad fixture passes or a valid fixture depends on row order or hidden input.

title: Add DuckDB projection proof for traceable decision log JSONL
decision: Add a governance proposal and follow-up implementation path for DuckDB views/fixtures that read traceable decision log JSONL and project trace coverage, hard errors, deterministic digest, and closure violations. Keep DuckDB output non-authority and link back to adrs #19.

expected: A later governance implementation PR can add DuckDB SQL/views plus fixtures so valid trace JSONL passes and missing/dangling/non-deterministic/score-authority cases fail before governance projection is considered closed.

conditions: dangling edge count is zero for valid fixture
projection uses explicit input file and projector version only
DuckDB artifacts are rebuildable and non-authority
proof summary references adrs #19 and fixture digest
